Practical Program Evaluation Chapter Map

Full reading map for Practical Program Evaluation chapter notes.

Source

This map uses selected MCP research-library sections from Practical Program Evaluation (Chen 2015).

Reading Route

Foundations

  • Chapter 1: Fundamentals of Program Evaluation - pages 23-56; Chapter 1: Fundamentals of Program Evaluation
  • Chapter 2: Understand Approaches to Evaluation and Select Ones That Work: The Comprehensive Evaluation Typology - pages 57-78; Chapter 2: Understand Approaches to Evaluation and Select Ones That Work: the Comprehensive Evaluation Typology
  • Chapter 3: Logic Models and the Action Model/Change Model Schema (Program Theory) - pages 79-114; Chapter 3: Logic Models and the Action Model/change Model Schema (program Theory)

Planning

  • Chapter 4: Helping Stakeholders Clarify a Program Plan: Program Scope - pages 117-137; Chapter 4: Helping Stakeholders Clarify a Program Plan: Program Scope
  • Chapter 5: Helping Stakeholders Clarify a Program Plan: Action Plan - pages 139-171; Chapter 5: Helping Stakeholders Clarify a Program Plan: Action Plan

Implementation and Monitoring

  • Chapter 6: Constructive Process Evaluation Tailored for the Initial Implementation - pages 174-196; Chapter 6: Constructive Process Evaluation Tailored for the Initial Implementation
  • Chapter 7: Assessing Implementation in the Mature Implementation Stage - pages 197-220; Chapter 7: Assessing Implementation in the Mature Implementation Stage
  • Chapter 8: Program Monitoring and the Development of a Monitoring System - pages 223-250; Chapter 8: Program Monitoring and the Development of a Monitoring System

Outcome Evaluation

  • Chapter 9: Constructive Outcome Evaluations - pages 252-267; Chapter 9: Constructive Outcome Evaluations
  • Chapter 10: The Experimentation Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation - pages 268-280; Chapter 10: The Experimentation Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation
  • Chapter 11: The Holistic Effectuality Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation - pages 283-324; Chapter 11: The Holistic Effectuality Evaluation Approach to Outcome Evaluation
  • Chapter 12: The Theory-driven Approach to Outcome Evaluation - pages 325-360; Chapter 12: The Theory-driven Approach to Outcome Evaluation

Advanced Issues and Dissemination

  • Chapter 13: What to Do If Your Logic Model Does Not Work as Well as Expected - pages 364-385; Chapter 13: What to Do If Your Logic Model Does Not Work as Well as Expected
  • Chapter 14: Formal Theories versus Stakeholder Theories in Interventions: Relative Strengths and Limitations - pages 387-410; Chapter 14: Formal Theories versus Stakeholder Theories in Interventions: Relative Strengths and Limitations
  • Chapter 15: Evaluation and Dissemination: Top-down Approach versus Bottom-up Approach - pages 413-432; Chapter 15: Evaluation and Dissemination: Top-down Approach versus Bottom-up Approach

How to Use This Map

  • Start with Chapters 1-3 to select the evaluation route and clarify program theory.
  • Use Chapters 4-5 when the program is still being planned and needs program-scope or action-plan work.
  • Use Chapters 6-8 for early implementation, mature implementation, and monitoring-system questions.
  • Use Chapters 9-12 for outcome readiness, causal rigor, real-world effectuality, and theory-driven explanation.
  • Use Chapters 13-15 when logic models, theory sources, or dissemination assumptions need more advanced treatment.

Legacy Extracts

  • Evaluation Design and Its Components
  • Logic Models and the Action Model/Change Model Schema
  • When Logic Models Do Not Work as Expected

References

Chen, Huey T. 2015. Practical Program Evaluation. SAGE Publications.
